title: Self-Regulation & Resilience
objective: Students will explore self-regulation & resilience concepts including Personal Coping Toolkit, Resilience and Bouncing Back, Personal Goal-Setting and more.

steps.content: Let's explore Personal Coping Toolkit! Have you ever wondered about this?
Reflect on which self-regulation and coping strategies work best for them personally, building a 'toolkit' of approaches they can draw on in different situations and sharing what works with others
Let me show you something cool. Describe at least three personal coping strategies and when each is most effective
Can you tell you 'When I'm stressed about exams I go for a walk and that helps, but when I'm sad I need to talk to someone' — showing they know their own coping toolkit?

Great job! Today we learned about Personal Coping Toolkit. Describe at least three personal coping strategies and when each is most effective Recommend a strategy to a friend based on the friend's specific situation
Today we're going to learn about Resilience and Bouncing Back. Get ready for an adventure!

Understand resilience as the ability to recover from setbacks, adapt to difficult circumstances, and keep going — recognising that resilience is a skill that develops through experience, not a trait you either have or don't
Let me show you something cool. Define resilience in their own words and explain why it matters
After a real disappointment — like not getting a part in the school play or failing a test they studied for — can you feel upset but then pick themselves up and figure out what to do next?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Resilience and Bouncing Back: Define resilience in their own words and explain why it matters Describe a time they bounced back from a setback and what helped them recover
Let's discover the amazing world of Personal Goal-Setting!
Set realistic personal goals, create a simple plan to achieve them, monitor their own progress, and adjust their approach when things aren't working
Let me show you something cool. Write or state a specific, achievable personal goal with a timeframe
Can you set themselves a goal — like improving their swimming or finishing a book series — and actually work towards it step by step, adjusting their plan if needed?
Great job! Today we learned about Personal Goal-Setting. Write or state a specific, achievable personal goal with a timeframe Describe the steps they will take to reach the goal
Let's explore Time and Attention Management! Have you ever wondered about this?
Manage their own time and attention effectively — prioritising tasks, minimising distractions, and maintaining focus on important work even when it's not the most exciting option
Time to try this out: Create a simple plan for completing multiple tasks in a sensible order
When you has homework to finish but also wants to play a game, can they manage their time — doing the homework first or setting a timer — rather than always needing you to enforce it?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Time and Attention Management: Create a simple plan for completing multiple tasks in a sensible order Identify their own common distractions and describe strategies to manage them
